2026-06-05.patch_summary: This patch introduces a per-page_counter stock to the Linux kernel's memory management system, allowing each page counter to have its own cache of pre-charged pages. This addresses two issues: the random eviction of memcgs when handling more than 7 memcgs, and the tight coupling between stock and memcg, which prevents new page counters from using separate stocks. The patch adds a generic per-cpu stock struct and its allocation, disabling, and freeing functions, but does not yet use these features.
